description: Declares what 'how we work' config is mirrored between Jay (<<MACHINE_1_ID>>, $HOME) and Jarry (<<MACHINE_2_ID>>, $HOME) so nothing drifts or gets lost between the two machines. Consumed by scripts/mirror-check.sh (read-only drift report, wired into the session-init hook) and scripts/mirror-apply.sh (idempotent heal). The repo is the single source of truth; live locations are symlinks into it or deep-merges of a canonical shared file. ~ expands per-machine; repo-relative paths resolve against REPO_ROOT. This covers BOTH cross-machine sync (the two Macs) and cross-provider adapters (Claude Code under ClaudeCode/.claude, Codex under .codex/) — the analogous cross-PROVIDER parity check is scripts/platform-parity-check.py, driven by docs/process/platform-registry.json.

symlinks.note: PreToolUse(Edit|Write) redaction guard — denies money amounts in personal-log / #personal memory writes (tiered-context enforcement-hardening, closes the §4 redaction gap).
Provider-neutral root workspace contract. Keep Ant's home-level AGENTS.md symlinked so Codex/Desktop contexts and other AGENTS-aware tools read the Metis OS contract, not a stale copy.
Codex native AGENTS entrypoint. Mirrors the Metis OS neutral contract so Codex stays aligned with the same provider-neutral lifecycle as Claude Code and future drivers.
Codex CLI global context — machine-agnostic, shared across Jay and Jarry
Codex CLI command surface — lifecycle adapters (/start /next /free-work /checkpoint /end) mirroring the canonical Claude commands. Parity enforced by scripts/platform-parity-check.py.
Codex native hook registrations. Command trust is still machine-local in ~/.codex/config.toml, but the hook list itself is repo-owned and self-healed by mirror.py/bootstrap.

Codex user/repo skill discovery surface. Points to the repo-generated .agents/skills directory, which contains per-skill symlinks into ClaudeCode/skills so Codex and Claude share workflow bodies without making the Claude skill root a Codex cache.
Unify Codex memory into the SHARED agent RAG store so learning compounds across providers instead of forking into a Codex-private silo (multi-provider-agent-framework.md §6).

tomlMerge.note: Enforce repo-wide Codex permission defaults while preserving machine-local plugin/MCP/hook-trust config.

codexSurface.note: Generated/symlinked Codex surfaces derived from ClaudeCode/skills and ClaudeCode/commands. mirror.py check/apply runs the sync script so future Claude workflows appear in Codex automatically. .agents/skills is a real directory with per-skill symlinks, not a root symlink, so Codex cache folders do not pollute ClaudeCode/skills.

perMachineSymlinks.note: Claude Code native subagent personas (forge/scout/shield/echo/main). Target differs per machine — each dir has machine-specific Host Machine header but same role definitions. Wired by bootstrap-claude-memory.sh.

merge.note: Deep-merge: keys in settings.shared.json are forced to match on both machines; everything in localKeys is preserved from each machine's own file and never overwritten. statusLine is now canonical (~/.claude/statusline.sh on both machines).

checkOnly.note: ~/.claude/projects/<project>/memory must be a symlink to ClaudeCode/memory (wired by scripts/bootstrap-claude-memory.sh, not by mirror-apply because the <project> dir name is per-machine). mirror-check only verifies it resolves into the repo; if not, run bootstrap-claude-memory.sh.

neverMirror: LaunchAgent .plist absolute paths — generated per-machine from scripts/*.plist

modelHost.rule: Jay is the SOLE local-model host (Ollama qwen3-coder:30b). Jarry (32GB) must NOT run a local 30B model; its openclaw lanes route to Jay's Ollama via models.providers.ollama.baseUrl in ~/.openclaw/openclaw.json (the gateway ignores OLLAMA_HOST env var entirely). mirror-check warns if Jarry's lanes point at a paid model or localhost instead of Jay.
